mnemonics.title: Remember pclose Purpose
mnemonics.content: pclose = pipe + close + wait + collect status. Think: 'Parents Close Pipe, Children Complete' - Parent closes pipe and waits for child to complete.

mnemonics.title: popen Modes
mnemonics.content: 'r' = read FROM command, 'w' = write TO command. Remember: First letter matches direction FROM program's perspective.

mnemonics.title: pclose Return Values
mnemonics.content: pclose returns: Success = exit status (use WEXITSTATUS), Error = -1. Think: 'Status or -1, No Zero Here!'

acronyms.usage: Pipes are a form of IPC enabling data transfer between processes
Creates a pipe and executes a command, returning a FILE pointer
Closes a pipe opened by popen and waits for command completion
Terminated process whose exit status hasn't been collected by parent

visualTips.visualization: Draw a timeline: popen() → fork child → exec command → parent reads/writes pipe → pclose() → waitpid collects status → process exits. Think of it as a pipeline connecting two rooms.
Picture a water pipe: popen('r') = water flows FROM command TO program (reading); popen('w') = water flows FROM program TO command (writing). Arrow direction = where data GOES.
Process states as boxes: Created → Running → Terminated (Zombie) → Collected. pclose bridges Running to Collected, preventing Zombie.

analogies.analogy: Like borrowing and returning a library book - popen() 'borrows' the command's output stream, pclose() 'returns' it and signs off on the transaction.
Think of a pipe as a conveyor belt between a factory (command) and warehouse (program). popen() starts the belt, pclose() stops it and checks the final delivery.
A zombie is like a completed delivery truck waiting at the dock - the job is done but no one has signed the delivery receipt. pclose() is the receipt signature.
